commands.review_summary:
The PR refactors Active Memory recall result handling so a completed sub-agent summary can override an early terminal `memory_search` unavailable trace, filters diagnostic-only text, and adds focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +63, Tests +118. Total +181 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was established in this review. Current main source shows the terminal unavailable branch can win before a later sub-agent result, and the PR adds focused regression tests for that race.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The next action is contributor real behavior proof plus maintainer review; there is no narrow ClawSweeper code repair to queue from this review.

target.title: fix(active-memory): preserve verbose recall summaries
target.body:
﻿## Summary

- Preserve successful verbose Active Memory sub-agent summaries when an early terminal `memory_search` unavailable trace races the embedded run result.
- Keep true unavailable diagnostics fail-closed: if the recovered payload is only the memory-search warning/action/error text, it is not injected into the prompt.
- Add regressions for both the verbose-summary recovery path and the diagnostics-only fallback path.

Why does this matter now?

#90454 reports bundled `active-memory` dropping successful provider responses as `status=unavailable`, which makes memory context disappear and sends operators toward provider/cooldown debugging even when the provider returned HTTP 200.

What is intentionally out of scope?

No config changes, no provider routing changes, no changelog edit.

What should reviewers focus on?

The terminal memory-search race in `maybeResolveActiveRecall`, especially that useful assistant text wins while diagnostic-only text still stays unavailable.

## Linked context

Closes #90454

Related #90454

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Picked up from the specific issue queue and ClawSweeper's assessment on #90454.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `active-memory` no longer turns a successful verbose sub-agent summary into `status=unavailable` solely because a terminal unavailable memory-search trace was observed first.
- Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux, `provider=aws`, lease `cbx_c3567832126b`, run `run_15a81c954bcf`, Node `v24.13.0`, branch commit `82be2f9402`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: cloned `openclaw/openclaw`, fetched `brokemac79:fix/issue-90454-active-memory-verbose`, installed with `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, then ran `env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts`.
- Evidence after fix: Crabbox run `run_15a81c954bcf` completed with `extension-active-memory extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts (131 tests)` and `131 passed`.
- Observed result after fix: the new verbose-summary regression returns `status=ok`; the diagnostics-only regression remains `status=unavailable` and injects no memory context.
- What was not tested: no live Anthropic account/provider-console replay was run from this environment.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: local Windows Vitest reaches selected test teardown but fails on pre-existing temp SQLite `EBUSY` cleanup; Linux Crabbox is the authoritative proof for this PR. Mantis was not available on PATH.
- Before evidence: #90454 includes the reported gateway/provider-console correlation; ClawSweeper also identified the terminal unavailable race as the likely current-main path needing regression coverage.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/active-memory/index.ts extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Local Windows: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts -- -t "uses verbose assistant text that arrives after an unavailable memory_search trace|does not recover unavailable diagnostics as memory context|fast-fails unavailable memory_search results"` reached test teardown, then failed on temp SQLite `EBUSY` cleanup.
- Crabbox AWS focused proof: `run_304324d82c97`, lease `cbx_9637e6ce3a63`, 2 selected tests passed before the Codex-review follow-up.
- Crabbox AWS full proof after the final patch: `run_15a81c954bcf`, lease `cbx_c3567832126b`, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts`, 131 passed.
- `codex review --base origin/main`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added coverage for verbose assistant text arriving shortly after an unavailable `memory_search` transcript record.
- Added coverage ensuring the unavailable diagnostic warning itself is not recovered as prompt context.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The issue scenario would return/persist `status=unavailable` before the sub-agent's non-empty summary payload could be harvested.

If no test was added, why not?

Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Active Memory can now inject a recovered non-empty summary in a race that previously returned unavailable.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Accidentally treating provider/tool diagnostic text as recalled memory context.

How is that risk mitigated?

Recovered summaries are filtered against the unavailable warning/action/error text, and the diagnostics-only regression locks that behavior.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Ready for maintainer review after CI and ClawSweeper run on the PR.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI and ClawSweeper PR review are pending after PR creation. Live Anthropic/provider-console replay was not available here; Crabbox regression proof is included.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Local Codex review initially flagged diagnostic text recovery risk. The final `codex review --base origin/main` reported no actionable correctness issues.
